html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty-Four — Part One

Divrei HaYamim Aleph

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH

Covenant History — The Genealogical Foundation

Chapters 1 through 9

Adam to Avraham — The Line of Dawid — The Prayer of Yavetz — The Sons of Levi — The Levitical Singers — The First to Return

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Orit Ge'ez — Ethiopian Complete Canon

Masoretic Text

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 9 — THE COVENANT GENEALOGICAL FOUNDATION

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 1 Adam to Noakh — The Sons of Noakh — From Shem to Avram — The Sons of Yishma'el — The Sons of Keturah — The Kings of Edom — The Chiefs of Edom

1 Adam, Shet, Enosh,

2 Kenan, Mahalal'el, Yered,

3 Khanokh, Metushelakh, Lamekh,

4 Noakh, Shem, Kham, and Yefet.

5 The sons of Yefet: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Yavan, Tuval, Meshekh, and Tiras.

6 The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Rifat, and Togarmah.

7 The sons of Yavan: Elisha, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.

8 The sons of Kham: Kush, Mitzrayim, Put, and Kena'an.

9 The sons of Kush: Seva, Havilah, Savta, Ra'ma, and Savtekha. The sons of Ra'ma: Sheva and Dedan.

10 Kush fathered Nimrod. He was the first on earth to be a mighty man.

11 Mitzrayim fathered the Ludim, the Anamim, the Lehavim, the Naftuhim,

12 The Patrusim, the Kasluhim — from whom the Pelishtim came — and the Kaftorim.

13 Kena'an fathered Tzidon his firstborn and Khet,

14 And the Yevusite, the Amorite, the Girgashite,

15 The Hivite, the Arkite, the Sinite,

16 The Arvadite, the Tzemarite, and the Khamathite.

17 The sons of Shem: Eilam, Assyria, Arpakhshad, Lud, Aram, Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshekh.

18 Arpakhshad fathered Shelakh, and Shelakh fathered Ever.

19 To Ever were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg — for in his days the earth was divided — and his brother's name was Yoktan.

20 Yoktan fathered Almodad, Shelef, Hatzarmaveth, Yerakh,

21 Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,

22 Eval, Avima'el, Sheva,

23 Ofir, Havilah, and Yovav. All these were the sons of Yoktan.

24 Shem, Arpakhshad, Shelakh,

25 Ever, Peleg, Re'u,

26 Serug, Nakhor, Terakh,

27 Avram, that is Avraham.

28 The sons of Avraham: Yitzkhak and Yishma'el.

29 These are their genealogies: the firstborn of Yishma'el, Nevayot, then Kedar, Adve'el, Mivsam,

30 Mishma, Dumah, Massa, Hadad, Tema,

31 Yetur, Nafish, and Kedmah. These are the sons of Yishma'el.

32 The sons of Keturah, Avraham's concubine: she bore Zimran, Yokshan, Medan, Midyan, Yishbak, and Shuakh. The sons of Yokshan: Sheva and Dedan.

33 The sons of Midyan: Efah, Efer, Khanokh, Avida, and Elda'ah. All these were the sons of Keturah.

34 Avraham fathered Yitzkhak. The sons of Yitzkhak: Esav and Yisra'EL.

35 The sons of Esav: Elifaz, Re'uel, Ye'ush, Ya'lam, and Korakh.

36 The sons of Elifaz: Teman, Omar, Tzfei, Gatam, Kenaz, Timna, and Amalek.

37 The sons of Re'uel: Nakhat, Zerakh, Shammah, and Mizzah.

38 The sons of Se'ir: Lotan, Shoval, Tziv'on, Anah, Dishon, Etzer, and Dishan.

39 The sons of Lotan: Hori and Homam. And Lotan's sister was Timna.

40 The sons of Shoval: Alyan, Manakhat, Eval, Shfei, and Onam. The sons of Tziv'on: Ayah and Anah.

41 The son of Anah: Dishon. The sons of Dishon: Hamran, Eshban, Yitran, and Keran.

42 The sons of Etzer: Bilhan, Za'avan, and Ya'akan. The sons of Dishan: Utz and Aran.

43 These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the people of Yisra'EL: Bela the son of Be'or, the name of his city being Dinhabah.

44 Bela died, and Yovav the son of Zerakh of Botzrah reigned in his place.

45 Yovav died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

46 Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated Midyan in the country of Moav, reigned in his place, the name of his city being Avit.

47 Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.

48 Samlah died, and Shaul of Rehovot on the Euphrates reigned in his place.

49 Shaul died, and Ba'al-Khanan the son of Akhbor reigned in his place.

50 Ba'al-Khanan died, and Hadad reigned in his place, the name of his city being Pa'i, and his wife's name Mehetavel the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mei-Zahav.

51 And Hadad died. The chiefs of Edom were: chief Timna, chief Alyah, chief Yetet,

52 Chief Oholivamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon,

53 Chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mivtzar,

54 Chief Magdi'el, and chief Iram. These are the chiefs of Edom.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No substantial Divrei HaYamim manuscript was found at Qumran. The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic traditions are the primary authorities for this volume. Divrei HaYamim opens with the most extensive genealogical compilation in the covenant canon — nine chapters of covenant genealogy from Adam to the first return from exile. The covenant purpose of this genealogical foundation is to establish the covenant community's covenant continuity with the entire covenant history from creation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. The genealogy from Adam to Avraham — verses 1-27 — is the covenant assertion that the covenant community of Yisra'EL stands within the covenant universal history of all humanity. Avraham is not the beginning of covenant history but the covenant focal point within a covenant history that begins with Adam. The covenant universal and the covenant particular are held together from the first chapter.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 1 as the foundation of the covenant genealogical survey. The Edomite king list of verses 43-50 is the covenant historical record of the covenant community's covenant neighbors before the covenant Israelite monarchy. The covenant pre-Israelite kings of Edom are covenant documented because the covenant relationship between the descendants of Yaakov and Esav is a covenant continuous covenant narrative.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The genealogy of Chapter 1 follows the structure of Bereshit 5, 10, and 25, drawing from the covenant foundational texts of the Torah to establish the covenant chain from Adam to Avraham to Yitzkhak to Esav and Yisra'EL. The covenant compiler of Divrei HaYamim uses the covenant genealogical form as the covenant narrative vehicle for establishing covenant communal identity: who we are is inseparable from who we came from.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 2 The Sons of Yisra'EL — Yehudah and Tamar — The Line of Ram to Dawid — The Sons of Khevroni — The Sons of Kalev — The Sons of Yerakhmeel

1 These are the sons of Yisra'EL: Re'uven, Shim'on, Levi, Yehudah, Yissachar, Zevulun,

2 Dan, Yosef, Binyamin, Naftali, Gad, and Asher.

3 The sons of Yehudah: Er, Onan, and Shelah. These three were born to him by Bat-Shua the Kena'anite. And Er, Yehudah's firstborn, was evil in the sight of YHWH, and he put him to death.

4 And Tamar his daughter-in-law bore him Peretz and Zerakh. All the sons of Yehudah were five.

5 The sons of Peretz: Khetzron and Hamul.

6 The sons of Zerakh: Zimri, Eitan, Heman, Kalkol, and Dara, five in all.

7 The son of Karmi: Akhar, the troubler of Yisra'EL, who broke faith in the matter of the devoted thing.

8 The son of Eitan: Azaryahu.

9 The sons of Khetzron that were born to him: Yerakhmeel, Ram, and Keluvai.

10 Ram fathered Amminadav, and Amminadav fathered Nakhshon, prince of the sons of Yehudah.

11 Nakhshon fathered Salma, and Salma fathered Bo'az,

12 Bo'az fathered Oved, and Oved fathered Yishai.

13 Yishai fathered Eliav his firstborn, Avinadav the second, Shim'a the third,

14 Netanel the fourth, Raddai the fifth,

15 Otzzem the sixth, Dawid the seventh.

16 And their sisters were Tzeruyah and Avigayil. The sons of Tzeruyah: Avishai, Yo'av, and Asahel, three.

17 Avigayil bore Amasa, and the father of Amasa was Yeter the Yishma'elite.

18 Kalev the son of Khetzron fathered children by his wife Azuvah, and by Yeriot. And these were her sons: Yesher, Shovav, and Ardon.

19 When Azuvah died, Kalev married Efrat, who bore him Hur.

20 Hur fathered Uri, and Uri fathered Bezalel.

21 Afterward Khetzron went in to the daughter of Makhir the father of Gil'ad, whom he married when he was sixty years old, and she bore him Seguv.

22 And Seguv fathered Yair, who had twenty-three cities in the land of Gil'ad.

23 But Geshur and Aram took from them Havot-Yair, Kenat, and its villages — sixty towns. All these were the sons of Makhir the father of Gil'ad.

24 After the death of Khetzron, Kalev went in to Efratah, the wife of Khetzron his father, and she bore him Ashkhur, the father of Tekoa.

25 The sons of Yerakhmeel the firstborn of Khetzron: Ram, his firstborn, Bunah, Oren, Otzzem, and Akhiyah.

26 Yerakhmeel had another wife, whose name was Atarah. She was the mother of Onam.

27 The sons of Ram, the firstborn of Yerakhmeel: Ma'atz, Yamin, and Eker.

28 The sons of Onam: Shammai and Yada. The sons of Shammai: Nadav and Avishur.

29 The name of Avishur's wife was Avikha'il, and she bore him Akhvan and Molid.

30 The sons of Nadav: Seled and Apayim. And Seled died without children.

31 The son of Apayim: Yishi. The son of Yishi: Sheshan. The son of Sheshan: Akhlai.

32 The sons of Yada, Shammai's brother: Yeter and Yonatan. And Yeter died without children.

33 The sons of Yonatan: Pelet and Zaza. These were the sons of Yerakhmeel.

34 Now Sheshan had no sons, only daughters, but Sheshan had an Egyptian slave whose name was Yarkha.

35 So Sheshan gave his daughter in marriage to Yarkha his slave, and she bore him Attai.

36 Attai fathered Natan, and Natan fathered Zavad.

37 Zavad fathered Eflal, and Eflal fathered Oved.

38 Oved fathered Yehu, and Yehu fathered Azaryahu.

39 Azaryahu fathered Khaletz, and Khaletz fathered El'asah.

40 El'asah fathered Sismai, and Sismai fathered Shallum.

41 Shallum fathered Yekamiahu, and Yekamiahu fathered Elishama.

42 The sons of Kalev the brother of Yerakhmeel: Mesha his firstborn, who was the father of Zif, and his son Mareshah the father of Khevron.

43 The sons of Khevron: Korakh, Tappuakh, Rekem, and Shema.

44 Shema fathered Rakham, the father of Yorke'am, and Rekem fathered Shammai.

45 The son of Shammai: Ma'on, and Ma'on was the father of Beit-Tzur.

46 Efah also, Kalev's concubine, bore Kharan, Moza, and Gazez. Kharan fathered Gazez.

47 The sons of Yahdai: Regem, Yotam, Geshan, Pelet, Efah, and Sha'af.

48 Ma'akha, Kalev's concubine, bore Shever and Tirkhana.

49 She also bore Sha'af the father of Madmannah, Sheva the father of Makhvena and the father of Givea. And the daughter of Kalev was Akhsah.

50 These were the descendants of Kalev. The sons of Hur the firstborn of Efratah: Shoval the father of Kiryat-Ye'arim,

51 Salma the father of Beit-Lekhem, and Kharef the father of Beit-Gader.

52 Shoval the father of Kiryat-Ye'arim had sons: Haro'eh, half of the Menukhotites.

53 And the clans of Kiryat-Ye'arim: the Yitrites, the Putites, the Shumatites, and the Mishraites. From these came the Tzorathites and the Eshtaolites.

54 The sons of Salma: Beit-Lekhem, the Netofathites, Atrot-Beit-Yo'av, and half of the Manakhtites, the Tzori'ites.

55 The clans also of the scribes who lived at Yavetz: the Tirathites, the Shim'athites, and the Sukathites. These are the Kenites who came from Khamat, the father of the house of Rekhav.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 2 establishes the genealogy of Yehudah — the covenant tribe from which Dawid descends — in full detail. The covenant line from Peretz through Khetzron through Ram to Amminadav, Nakhshon, Salma, Boaz, Oved, Yishai, and Dawid — verses 10-15 — is the covenant Davidic covenant line that undergirds the covenant messianic expectations of the entire prophetic corpus.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 3-4 — Yehudah, Tamar, Peretz, and Zerakh. The covenant inclusion of Tamar's story in the covenant genealogy is the covenant honest covenant record: the covenant Davidic line passes through a covenant story of covenant failure and covenant unexpected covenant faithfulness. The covenant lineage of the covenant king runs through covenant human complexity.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 15 — Dawid the seventh. The covenant position of Dawid in the covenant family of Yishai is documented as the seventh son — the covenant number of covenant completeness. The covenant seventh son who is the covenant last and covenant least expected — as Shemu'el Aleph 16 recounts — is the covenant chosen covenant king.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 34-35 — Sheshan had no sons, only daughters, and gave his daughter to his Egyptian slave Yarkha, and she bore him Attai. The covenant genealogy honestly records covenant social complexity: the covenant line continues through a covenant union between a covenant Israelite woman and an Egyptian slave. The covenant genealogy is not idealized — it is covenant honest about the covenant realities of covenant community life across covenant generations.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 3 The Sons of Dawid — Born in Khevron — Born in Yerushalayim — The Sons of Shelomoh — The Line of Yehoyakim — The Sons of Yekhonyahu — The Sons of Zerubavel

1 These are the sons of Dawid who were born to him in Khevron: the firstborn, Amnon, by Akhinoam the Yizre'elite; the second, Dani'el, by Avigayil the Karmelite;

2 The third, Avshalom, the son of Ma'akha the daughter of Talmai king of Geshur; the fourth, Adoniyahu, the son of Haggit;

3 The fifth, Shefatyahu, by Avital; the sixth, Yitream, by his wife Eglah.

4 Six were born to him in Khevron, where he reigned for seven years and six months. And he reigned thirty-three years in Yerushalayim.

5 These were born to him in Yerushalayim: Shim'a, Shovav, Natan, and Shelomoh, four by Bat-Shu'a the daughter of Ammi'el.

6 And Yivkhar, Elishama, Elifelet,

7 Nogah, Nefeg, Yafia,

8 Elishama, Elyada, and Elifelet, nine.

9 All these were Dawid's sons, besides the sons of the concubines, and Tamar was their sister.

10 The son of Shelomoh: Rekhavam, Aviyah his son, Asa his son, Yehoshafat his son,

11 Yoram his son, Akhaziyahu his son, Yo'ash his son,

12 Amatziyahu his son, Azaryahu his son, Yotam his son,

13 Akhaz his son, Khizkiyahu his son, Menasheh his son,

14 Amon his son, Yoshiyahu his son.

15 The sons of Yoshiyahu: Yokhanan the firstborn, the second Yehoiakim, the third Tzidkiyahu, the fourth Shallum.

16 The descendants of Yehoiakim: Yekhonyahu his son, Tzidkiyahu his son.

17 The sons of Yekhonyahu, the captive: She'altiel his son,

18 Malkiram, Pedayahu, Shenazzar, Yekamyah, Hoshama, and Nedavyah.

19 The sons of Pedayahu: Zerubavel and Shim'i. The son of Zerubavel: Meshullam and Hananyahu, and Shelomit was their sister.

20 And Hashuvah, Ohel, Berekhyahu, Khasadyahu, and Yushav-Khesed, five.

21 The sons of Hananyahu: Pelatyahu and Yeshayahu, his son Refayahu, his son Arnan, his son Ovadyahu, his son Shekhanyahu.

22 The son of Shekhanyahu: Shemayahu. And the sons of Shemayahu: Khattush, Yig'al, Bariyakh, Ne'aryahu, and Shafat, six.

23 The son of Ne'aryahu: Elyoe'nai, Hizkiyahu, and Azrikam, three.

24 The sons of Elyoe'nai: Hodavyahu, Elyashiv, Pelatyahu, Akkuv, Yohanan, Delayahu, and Anani, seven.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 3 is the covenant royal genealogy of Dawid's descendants — the covenant list that establishes the covenant Davidic succession from Dawid through the covenant monarchy of Yehudah to the covenant exile and beyond into the covenant post-exilic period with Zerubavel. The covenant genealogy extends the covenant Davidic line past the covenant exile into the covenant restoration period.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 17-19 — Yekhonyahu the captive, She'altiel, Pedayahu, Zerubavel. The covenant post-exilic Davidic line: Zerubavel — the covenant governor of the covenant first return — is documented as the covenant grandson of Yekhonyahu the covenant exiled king. The covenant royal line that was carried to Babylon is the covenant royal line that returns to rebuild the covenant temple.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 3 in full. The covenant list of covenant kings of Yehudah — verses 10-15 — documents the entire covenant Davidic dynasty from Shelomoh through Yoshiyahu. The covenant compiler traces the covenant royal line not to celebrate covenant royal covenant achievement but to document the covenant covenant continuity that survived even the covenant exile.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — Yekhonyahu the captive. The covenant designation of Yekhonyahu as captive — assir — marks the covenant genealogical transition from covenant free covenant monarchy to covenant exiled covenant royalty. The covenant genealogy does not end with the exile — it continues through it, documenting the covenant survival of the covenant Davidic line in covenant conditions of covenant captivity. The covenant line is unbroken by the covenant exile.

THE TRIBES OF YISRAEL — CHAPTERS 4 THROUGH 9

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 4 The Sons of Yehudah — The Sons of Kalar — The Prayer of Yavetz — The Sons of Ashhur — The Sons of Shim'on — The Sons of Shim'on — The Descendants of Shim'on

1 The sons of Yehudah: Peretz, Khetzron, Karmi, Hur, and Shoval.

2 Reayah the son of Shoval fathered Yakhat, and Yakhat fathered Akhumai and Lahad. These were the clans of the Tzorathites.

3 These were the sons of Etam: Yizre'el, Yishma, and Yivdash, and the name of their sister was Hatzlelponi.

4 And Penuel was the father of Gedor, and Ezer the father of Hushah. These were the sons of Hur, the firstborn of Efratah, the father of Beit-Lekhem.

5 Ashkhur, the father of Tekoa, had two wives, Khe'lah and Na'arah.

6 Na'arah bore him Akhuzzam, Khefer, Temeni, and Haashtari. These were the sons of Na'arah.

7 The sons of Khe'lah: Tzeret, Yitzhar, and Etnan.

8 Koz fathered Anuv, Hatzovevah, and the clans of Akharkhel the son of Harum.

9 Yavetz was more honorable than his brothers, and his mother called his name Yavetz, saying, Because I bore him in pain.

10 Yavetz called upon the Creator of Yisra'EL, saying, Oh that you would bless me and enlarge my border, and that your hand might be with me, and that you would keep me from harm so that it might not bring me pain. And Creator granted what he asked.

11 Kelub, the brother of Shukha, fathered Mehir, who was the father of Eshton.

12 Eshton fathered Beit-Rafa, Paseakh, and Tehinnah the father of Ir-Nakhash. These are the men of Rekhah.

13 The sons of Kenaz: Otni'el and Serayahu. The sons of Otni'el: Khatat and Me'onotai.

14 Me'onotai fathered Ofrah. Serayahu fathered Yo'av, the father of Ge-Kharashim, so called because they were craftsmen.

15 The sons of Kalev the son of Yefunneh: Iru, Elah, and Na'am. And the son of Elah: Kenaz.

16 The sons of Yehallelel: Zif, Zifah, Tirya, and Asarel.

17 The sons of Ezra: Yeter, Mered, Efer, and Yalon. She conceived and bore Miriam, Shammai, and Yishvakh the father of Eshtemoa.

18 And his Jewish wife bore Yered the father of Gedor, Khever the father of Sokho, and Yekuti'el the father of Zanoakh. These were the sons of Bitya the daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered married.

19 The sons of the wife of Hodiyah, the sister of Nakham: the father of Ke'ilah the Garmite and Eshtemoa the Ma'akhathite.

20 The sons of Shim'on: Amnon, Rinnah, Ben-Khanan, and Tilon. The sons of Yishi: Zokhet and Ben-Zokhet.

21 The sons of Shelah the son of Yehudah: Er the father of Lekha, and La'adah the father of Mareshah, and the clans of the house of the linen workers at Beit-Ashvea,

22 And Yokim, and the men of Kozeva, and Yo'ash, and Saraf, who ruled in Moav and returned to Lekhem — these are ancient records.

23 These were the potters and inhabitants of Neta'im and Gederah. They lived there in the king's service.

24 The sons of Shim'on: Nemuel, Yamin, Yariv, Zerakh, Shaul.

25 Shallum was his son, Mivsam his son, Mishma his son.

26 The sons of Mishma: Khammuel his son, Zakkur his son, Shim'i his son.

27 Shim'i had sixteen sons and six daughters. But his brothers did not have many children, nor did all their clan multiply like the people of Yehudah.

28 They lived in Be'er-Sheva, Moladah, Hatsar-Shu'al,

29 Bilhah, Etzem, Tolad,

30 Betu'el, Khormah, Ziklag,

31 Beit-Markavot, Hatsar-Susim, Beit-Bir'i, and Sha'arayim. These were their cities until Dawid became king.

32 And their villages were Etam, Ayin, Rimmon, Tokhen, and Ashan, five cities,

33 Along with all their villages that were around these cities as far as Ba'al. These were their settlements, and they kept a genealogical record.

34 And Meshobav, Yamlekh, Yoshah the son of Amatziyahu,

35 Yo'el, Yehu the son of Yoshivyahu, son of Serayahu, son of Asi'el,

36 Elyoe'nai, Ya'akova, Yeshokhayah, Asayahu, Adi'el, Yesimiel, Benayah,

37 Ziza the son of Shifi, son of Allon, son of Yedayah, son of Shimri, son of Shemayah —

38 These mentioned by name were princes in their clans, and their fathers' houses increased greatly.

39 They journeyed to the entrance of Gedor, to the east side of the valley, to seek pasture for their flocks.

40 Where they found rich, good pasture, and the land was very broad, quiet, and peaceful, for the former inhabitants there belonged to Kham.

41 These, registered by name, came in the days of Khizkiyahu king of Yehudah and attacked their tents and the Me'unites who were found there and destroyed them utterly to this day and settled in their place, because there was pasture there for their flocks.

42 And some of them, five hundred men of the Shim'onites, went to Mount Se'ir, having as their leaders Pelatyahu, Ne'aryahu, Refayahu, and Uzzi'el, the sons of Yishi.

43 And they defeated the remnant of the Amalekites who had escaped, and they have lived there to this day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 4 continues the genealogy of Yehudah with extended covenant family branches, including the famous covenant prayer of Yavetz — verse 9-10 — which interrupts the genealogical catalog with a covenant theological moment: the man whose name means pain asks YHWH to expand his covenant territory and keep him from covenant harm, and YHWH grants the covenant request.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 9-10 — Yavetz was more honorable than his brothers — he called on the Creator of Yisra'EL — and Creator granted what he asked. The covenant prayer of Yavetz embedded within the covenant genealogy is the covenant theological statement: within the covenant community, covenant honor is associated with covenant prayer and covenant trust in YHWH's covenant provision, not merely covenant genealogical covenant standing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 18 — the sons of the Jewish wife of Mered include those born to Bitya the daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered married. The covenant genealogy includes the covenant daughter of Pharaoh as a covenant ancestor of covenant Israelite clans — another covenant instance of covenant inclusive covenant genealogy that transcends covenant ethnic covenant boundaries.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 41-43 — the Shim'onites expand into territory formerly held by Kham's descendants and the remnant of Amalek. The covenant genealogy is simultaneously a covenant territorial record: the covenant tribal boundaries are determined by covenant historical covenant events, including covenant military covenant action that extended covenant tribal covenant possession into covenant previously held covenant foreign territories.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 5 The Sons of Re'uven — The Sons of Gad — They Cried Out to Creator in the Battle — The Half-Tribe of Menasheh — They Were Unfaithful — They Were Carried Into Exile

1 The sons of Re'uven the firstborn of Yisra'EL. He was the firstborn, but because he defiled his father's bed, his birthright was given to the sons of Yosef the son of Yisra'EL, so that he is not enrolled in the genealogy according to the birthright.

2 Though Yehudah became strong among his brothers and a prince was from him, yet the birthright belonged to Yosef.

3 The sons of Re'uven the firstborn of Yisra'EL: Khanokh, Pallu, Khetzron, and Karmi.

4 The sons of Yo'el: Shemayah his son, Gog his son, Shim'i his son,

5 Mikha his son, Re'ayah his son, Ba'al his son,

6 Be'erah his son, whom Tilgat-Piln'eser king of Assyria carried away into exile. He was a chief of the Re'uvenites.

7 And his kinsmen by their clans, when the genealogy of their generations was recorded: the chief, Ye'i'el, and Zekhariah,

8 And Bela the son of Azaz, son of Shema, son of Yo'el, who lived in Aro'er, as far as Nevo and Ba'al-Me'on.

9 He also lived to the east as far as the entrance of the desert this side of the Euphrates, because their livestock had multiplied in the land of Gil'ad.

10 And in the days of Sha'ul they waged war against the Hagarites, who fell into their hand. And they lived in their tents throughout all the region east of Gil'ad.

11 The sons of Gad lived over against them in the land of Bashan as far as Salkhah:

12 Yo'el the chief, Shafam the second, Yanai, and Shafat in Bashan.

13 And their kinsmen according to their fathers' houses: Mikha'el, Meshullam, Sheva, Yorai, Yakhan, Zia, and Ever, seven.

14 These were the sons of Avikha'il the son of Khuri, son of Yaro'akh, son of Gil'ad, son of Mikha'el, son of Yeshishai, son of Yakhdo, son of Buz.

15 Akhi the son of Avdi'el, son of Guni, was chief of their fathers' houses.

16 And they lived in Gil'ad, in Bashan and its villages, and in all the pasturelands of Sharon to their limits.

17 All of these were recorded in genealogies in the days of Yotam king of Yehudah and in the days of Yarov'am king of Yisra'EL.

18 The Re'uvenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Menasheh had valiant men who carried shield and sword, and drew the bow, expert in war, 44,760, able to go to war.

19 They waged war against the Hagarites, Yetur, Nafish, and Nodav.

20 And when they prevailed over them, the Hagarites and all who were with them were given into their hands, for they cried out to Creator in the battle, and he granted their urgent plea because they trusted in him.

21 They carried off their livestock: 50,000 of their camels, 250,000 sheep, 2,000 donkeys, and 100,000 men alive.

22 For many fell slain, because the war was from Creator. And they lived in their place until the exile.

23 The members of the half-tribe of Menasheh lived in the land. They were very numerous from Bashan to Ba'al-Hermon, Senir, and Mount Khermon.

24 These were the heads of their fathers' houses: Efer, Yishi, Eli'el, Azri'el, Yirmeyahu, Hodavyah, and Yakhdi'el, mighty warriors, famous men, heads of their fathers' houses.

25 But they broke faith with the Creator of their fathers and whored after the gods of the peoples of the land, whom Creator had destroyed before them.

26 So the Creator of Yisra'EL stirred up the spirit of Pul king of Assyria, the spirit of Tilgat-Pilneser king of Assyria, and he took them into exile, namely, the Re'uvenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Menasheh, and brought them to Halakh, Khavor, Hara, and the river Gozan, to this day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 5 documents the eastern covenant tribes — Re'uven, Gad, and half of Menasheh — and records their covenant defeat of the Hagarites through covenant prayer and covenant trust in YHWH — verse 20 — before documenting their covenant ultimate exile to Assyria because of covenant faithlessness to YHWH. The covenant pattern is consistent: covenant faithfulness produces covenant victory; covenant faithlessness produces covenant exile.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 20 — they cried out to Creator in the battle, and he granted their urgent plea because they trusted in him. The covenant explanation of the covenant military victory: not superior covenant military covenant capability but covenant prayer and covenant trust. The covenant theological criterion for covenant victory is stated explicitly: they trusted in him.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 1-2 — Re'uven's birthright was given to Yosef because he defiled his father's bed, yet Yehudah became strong among his brothers and a prince was from him. The covenant genealogical theology: covenant birthright does not simply follow covenant biological birth order. Covenant character — covenant faithfulness or covenant failure — shapes covenant genealogical covenant significance.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25-26 — they broke faith with the Creator of their fathers and whored after the gods of the peoples of the land — so Creator stirred up the spirit of the Assyrian king and he took them into exile. The covenant cause-and-effect theology of the covenant compiler: the covenant exile is not random covenant political disaster but the covenant consequence of covenant covenant-breaking. YHWH actively stirs the covenant imperial power as the covenant instrument of covenant judgment.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 6 The Sons of Levi — The High Priestly Line — The Levitical Singers Under Dawid — The Cities of the Levites

1 The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.

2 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Yitzhar, Khevron, and Uzzi'el.

3 The children of Amram: Aharon, Mosheh, and Miryam. The sons of Aharon: Nadav, Avihu, El'azar, and Itamar.

4 El'azar fathered Pinkhas, Pinkhas fathered Avishua,

5 Avishua fathered Bukki, Bukki fathered Uzzi,

6 Uzzi fathered Zerakhy ahu, Zerakhy ahu fathered Meraioth,

7 Meraioth fathered Amaryahu, Amaryahu fathered Akhituv,

8 Akhituv fathered Tzadok, Tzadok fathered Akhima'atz,

9 Akhima'atz fathered Azaryahu, Azaryahu fathered Yohanan,

10 Yohanan fathered Azaryahu — it was he who served as priest in the house that Shelomoh built in Yerushalayim.

11 Azaryahu fathered Amaryahu, Amaryahu fathered Akhituv,

12 Akhituv fathered Tzadok, Tzadok fathered Shallum,

13 Shallum fathered Khilkiyahu, Khilkiyahu fathered Azaryahu,

14 Azaryahu fathered Serayahu, Serayahu fathered Yehotzadak.

15 And Yehotzadak went into exile when YHWH sent Yehudah and Yerushalayim into exile by the hand of Nevukhadnetzar.

16 The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.

17 And these are the names of the sons of Gershom: Livni and Shim'i.

18 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Yitzhar, Khevron, and Uzzi'el.

19 The sons of Merari: Makhli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites according to their fathers.

20 Of Gershom: Livni his son, Yakhat his son, Zimmah his son,

21 Yo'akh his son, Iddo his son, Zerakh his son, Yeat'rai his son.

22 The sons of Kohath: Amminadav his son, Korakh his son, Assir his son,

23 Elkanah his son, Evyasaf his son, Assir his son,

24 Takhat his son, Uriel his son, Uziyahu his son, and Sha'ul his son.

25 The sons of Elkanah: Amasai and Akhimot.

26 As for Elkanah, the sons of Elkanah: Tzofai his son, Nakhat his son,

27 Eliav his son, Yeroham his son, Elkanah his son.

28 The sons of Shemu'el: Yo'el his firstborn, the second Aviyah.

29 The sons of Merari: Makhli, Livni his son, Shim'i his son, Uzza his son,

30 Shim'a his son, Khaggiyah his son, Asayahu his son.

31 These are the men whom Dawid put in charge of the service of song in the house of YHWH after the ark rested there.

32 They ministered with song before the tabernacle of the tent of meeting until Shelomoh built the house of YHWH in Yerushalayim, and they performed their service according to their order.

33 These are the men who served, and their sons. Of the sons of the Kohathites: Heman the singer, the son of Yo'el, son of Shemu'el,

34 Son of Elkanah, son of Yeroham, son of Eli'el, son of Toakh,

35 Son of Tzuf, son of Elkanah, son of Makhat, son of Amasai,

36 Son of Elkanah, son of Yo'el, son of Azaryahu, son of Tzefanyahu,

37 Son of Takhat, son of Assir, son of Evyasaf, son of Korakh,

38 Son of Yitzhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Yisra'EL.

39 And his brother Asaf, who stood on his right hand, namely Asaf the son of Berekhyahu, son of Shim'a,

40 Son of Mikha'el, son of Ba'aseiyahu, son of Malkiyahu,

41 Son of Etni, son of Zerakh, son of Adayahu,

42 Son of Eitan, son of Zimmah, son of Shim'i,

43 Son of Yakhat, son of Gershom, son of Levi.

44 On the left hand were their brothers the sons of Merari: Eitan the son of Kishi, son of Avdi, son of Mallukh,

45 Son of Khashavyahu, son of Amatzyahu, son of Khilkiyahu,

46 Son of Amtzi, son of Bani, son of Shemer,

47 Son of Makhli, son of Mushi, son of Merari, son of Levi.

48 And their brothers the Levites were appointed for all the service of the tabernacle of the house of Creator.

49 But Aharon and his sons made offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense for all the work of the most holy place, and to make atonement for Yisra'EL, according to all that Mosheh the servant of Creator had commanded.

50 These are the sons of Aharon: El'azar his son, Pinkhas his son, Avishua his son,

51 Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerakhy ahu his son,

52 Meraioth his son, Amaryahu his son, Akhituv his son,

53 Tzadok his son, Akhima'atz his son.

54 Now these are their dwelling places according to their settlements within their borders: to the sons of Aharon of the clans of Kohathites, for the lot fell to them,

55 To them they gave Khevron in the land of Yehudah and its surrounding pasturelands,

56 But the fields of the city and its villages they gave to Kalev the son of Yefunneh.

57 To the sons of Aharon they gave the cities of refuge: Khevron, Livnah with its pasturelands, Yattir, Eshtemoa with its pasturelands,

58 Khilen with its pasturelands, Devir with its pasturelands,

59 Ashan with its pasturelands, and Beit-Shemesh with its pasturelands.

60 And from the tribe of Binyamin: Geva with its pasturelands, Alemeth with its pasturelands, and Anatot with its pasturelands. All their cities throughout their clans were thirteen.

61 To the rest of the Kohathites were given by lot out of the clan of the tribe, out of the half-tribe, the half of Menasheh, ten cities.

62 To the Gershomites according to their clans were allotted thirteen cities out of the tribes of Yissachar, Asher, Naftali, and Menasheh in Bashan.

63 To the Merarites according to their clans were allotted twelve cities out of the tribes of Re'uven, Gad, and Zevulun.

64 So the people of Yisra'EL gave the Levites the cities with their pasturelands.

65 They gave by lot out of the tribes of Yehudah, Shim'on, and Binyamin these cities that are mentioned by name.

66 And some of the clans of the sons of Kohath had cities of their territory out of the tribe of Efrayim.

67 They were given the cities of refuge: Shekhem with its pasturelands in the hill country of Efrayim, Gezer with its pasturelands,

68 Yokmeam with its pasturelands, Beit-Khoron with its pasturelands,

69 Ayalon with its pasturelands, and Gat-Rimmon with its pasturelands.

70 And out of the half-tribe of Menasheh: Aner with its pasturelands, and Vileam with its pasturelands, for the rest of the clans of the Kohathites.

71 To the Gershomites were given out of the clan of the half-tribe of Menasheh: Golan in Bashan with its pasturelands and Ashtarot with its pasturelands.

72 And out of the tribe of Yissachar: Kedesh with its pasturelands, Davrat with its pasturelands,

73 Ramot with its pasturelands, and Anem with its pasturelands.

74 Out of the tribe of Asher: Mashal with its pasturelands, Avdon with its pasturelands,

75 Khukok with its pasturelands, and Rekhov with its pasturelands.

76 And out of the tribe of Naftali: Kedesh in Galil with its pasturelands, Khamon with its pasturelands, and Kiryataim with its pasturelands.

77 To the rest of the Merarites were allotted out of the tribe of Zevulun: Rimono with its pasturelands, Tabor with its pasturelands.

78 And beyond the Yarden at Yericho, on the east side of the Yarden, were given out of the tribe of Re'uven: Betzer in the wilderness with its pasturelands, Yahtzah with its pasturelands,

79 Kedemot with its pasturelands, and Mefaat with its pasturelands.

80 And out of the tribe of Gad: Ramot in Gil'ad with its pasturelands, Makhanayim with its pasturelands,

81 Kheshbon with its pasturelands, and Yazer with its pasturelands.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Chapter 6 is the longest chapter in Divrei HaYamim Aleph — the covenant genealogy and covenant geography of the Levites. The covenant Levitical cities — the covenant 48 cities designated in Bemidbar 35 for the covenant priestly tribe — are the covenant geographic framework of the covenant Levitical service throughout the covenant land. The covenant Levitical singers under Dawid — Heman, Asaf, and Eitan — are covenant identified with their full covenant genealogical lines.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 31-32 — Dawid put these men in charge of the service of song in the house of YHWH after the ark rested there. They ministered with song before the tabernacle until Shelomoh built the house of YHWH. The covenant institution of covenant Levitical music is attributed to Dawid's covenant initiative — the covenant musical tradition that produced the Tehillim is grounded in the covenant organizational covenant decision of the covenant king.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 49 — Aharon and his sons made offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense for all the work of the most holy place, and to make atonement for Yisra'EL, according to all that Mosheh the servant of Creator had commanded. The covenant priestly covenant function is explicitly grounded in the covenant Mosaic covenant commission: the covenant priesthood exercises covenant authorized covenant functions.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The genealogy of the covenant high priestly line — verses 1-15 — traces the covenant Aaronide covenant succession from Aharon through El'azar, Pinkhas, Avishua, Bukki, Uzzi, Zerakhy ahu, Meraioth, Amaryahu, Akhituv, Tzadok, Akhima'atz, Azaryahu, Amaryahu, Akhituv, Tzadok, Shallum, Khilkiyahu, Azaryahu, Serayahu, to Yehotzadak who went into exile. The covenant priestly line is documented through the covenant exile just as the covenant royal line was: the covenant exile does not end the covenant priestly line.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 7 The Sons of Yissachar — The Sons of Binyamin — The Sons of Naftali — The Sons of Menasheh — The Sons of Efrayim — The Sons of Asher

1 The sons of Yissachar: Tola, Puah, Yashuv, and Shimron, four.

2 The sons of Tola: Uzzi, Refayahu, Yeri'el, Yakhmai, Yivsam, and Shemu'el, heads of their fathers' houses. The sons of Tola were mighty warriors of their generations. Their number in the days of Dawid was 22,600.

3 The son of Uzzi: Yizrakhyahu. And the sons of Yizrakhyahu: Mikha'el, Ovadyahu, Yo'el, and Yishshiyahu, five, all of them chief men.

4 And along with them, by their generations, according to their fathers' houses, were 36,000 troops ready for war, for they had many wives and sons.

5 Their kinsmen belonging to all the clans of Yissachar were in all 87,000 mighty warriors, enrolled by genealogy.

6 The sons of Binyamin: Bela, Bekher, and Yedia'el, three.

7 The sons of Bela: Etzbon, Uzzi, Uzzi'el, Yerimot, and Iri, five, heads of fathers' houses, mighty warriors. And their enrollment by genealogy was 22,034.

8 The sons of Bekher: Zemira, Yo'ash, Eli'ezer, Elyoe'nai, Omri, Yeremot, Aviyah, Anatot, and Alemet. All these were the sons of Bekher.

9 And their enrollment by genealogy, according to their generations, as heads of their fathers' houses, mighty warriors, was 20,200.

10 The son of Yedia'el: Bilhan. And the sons of Bilhan: Ye'ush, Binyamin, Ehud, Khena'anah, Zeitan, Tarshish, and Akhishahar.

11 All these were the sons of Yedia'el according to the heads of their fathers' houses, mighty warriors, 17,200, fit for service in war.

12 And Shuppim and Khuppim were the sons of Ir, and Khushim the son of Akher.

13 The sons of Naftali: Yakhtze'el, Guni, Yetzer, and Shallum, the sons of Bilhah.

14 The sons of Menasheh: Asri'el, whom his Aramean concubine bore. She bore Makhir the father of Gil'ad.

15 And Makhir took a wife for Khuppim and Shuppim. The name of his sister was Ma'akhah. And the name of the second was Tzlofkhad, and Tzlofkhad had daughters.

16 And Ma'akhah the wife of Makhir bore a son, and she called his name Peresh. And the name of his brother was Sheresh, and his sons were Ulam and Rekem.

17 The son of Ulam: Bedan. These were the sons of Gil'ad the son of Makhir, son of Menasheh.

18 And his sister Molechet bore Ishhod, Avi'ezer, and Makhla.

19 The sons of Shemida were Akhyan, Shekhem, Likhi, and Aniam.

20 The sons of Efrayim: Shuthelakh, and Bered his son, Takhat his son, El'adah his son, Takhat his son,

21 Zavad his son, Shuthelakh his son, and Ezer and El'ad, whom the men of Gat who were born in that land killed, because they came down to raid their livestock.

22 And Efrayim their father mourned many days, and his brothers came to comfort him.

23 And Efrayim went in to his wife, and she conceived and bore a son. And he called his name Beri'ah, because disaster had befallen his house.

24 His daughter was She'erah, who built both lower and upper Beit-Khoron, and Uzzen-She'erah.

25 Refakh was his son, Reshef his son, Telakh his son, Takhan his son,

26 La'adan his son, Ammikhud his son, Elishama his son,

27 Nun his son, Yehoshua his son.

28 Their possessions and settlements were Beit-El and its towns, and to the east Na'aran, and to the west Gezer and its towns, Shekhem and its towns, and Ayah and its towns.

29 And along the borders of Menasheh, Beit-She'an and its towns, Ta'anakh and its towns, Megiddo and its towns, Dor and its towns. In these lived the sons of Yosef the son of Yisra'EL.

30 The sons of Asher: Yimnah, Yishvah, Yishvi, and Beriah, and their sister Serakh.

31 The sons of Beriah: Khever and Malki'el, who was the father of Birzaith.

32 Khever fathered Yaflet, Shomer, Khotam, and their sister Shua.

33 The sons of Yaflet: Pasakh, Vimhal, and Ashvat. These are the sons of Yaflet.

34 The sons of Shemer: Akhi, Rohgah, Jehubbah, and Aram.

35 The sons of his brother Khotem: Tzofakh, Yimna, Shelesh, and Amal.

36 The sons of Tzofakh: Suakh, Kharnefer, Shu'al, Beri, Yimrah,

37 Betzer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Yitran, and Be'era.

38 The sons of Yeter: Yefunneh, Pispah, and Ara.

39 The sons of Ulla: Arakh, Khanni'el, and Ritzya.

40 All of these were the sons of Asher, heads of fathers' houses, approved, mighty warriors, chief of the princes. Their number enrolled by genealogy for service in war was 26,000.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 7 documents the covenant genealogies of Yissachar, Binyamin, Naftali, Menasheh, Efrayim, and Asher. The covenant genealogy of Efrayim includes the covenant poignant covenant story of Ezer and El'ad killed by the men of Gat — verses 21-23 — and Efrayim's grief. Covenant genealogy is not merely covenant names but covenant stories of covenant loss and covenant continuation.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 24 — his daughter was She'erah, who built both lower and upper Beit-Khoron, and Uzzen-She'erah. The covenant record of a woman covenant builder within the covenant genealogical record: She'erah the daughter of Efrayim built covenant cities. The covenant genealogy honors covenant female covenant achievement within the covenant tribal covenant record.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 7 in full. The covenant military census data embedded in the covenant genealogy — 22,600 warriors from Tola, 87,000 from Yissachar — provides the covenant demographic foundation for understanding the covenant tribal covenant contributions to the covenant military and covenant national covenant life of Yisra'EL.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 21-23 — the sons of Efrayim were killed in a cattle raid against Gat, and Efrayim mourned many days. Then Efrayim went in to his wife, and she bore a son he called Beri'ah — disaster. The covenant genealogy preserves the covenant emotional reality behind the covenant names: Beri'ah's very covenant name commemorates the covenant disaster that preceded his birth. Covenant names in the covenant genealogy carry covenant memorial significance.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 8 The Sons of Binyamin — The Benjaminite Genealogy — The Sons of Sha'ul — The Gibeonite Mishpakha — The Descendants of Sha'ul in Yerushalayim

1 Binyamin fathered Bela his firstborn, Ashvel the second, Akharakh the third,

2 Nokha the fourth, and Rafa the fifth.

3 And Bela had sons: Addar, Gera, Avihud,

4 Avishua, Na'aman, Acho'akh,

5 Gera, Shefufan, and Khuram.

6 These are the sons of Ehud — these were the heads of fathers' houses of the inhabitants of Geva, and they were carried into exile to Manakhat:

7 Na'aman, Akhiyah, and Gera — that is, Heglam — who fathered Uzza and Akhikhud.

8 And Shaharayim fathered sons in the country of Moav after he had sent away Khushim and Ba'ara his wives.

9 He fathered sons by his wife Khodesh: Yovav, Tzivya, Mesha, Malkam,

10 Ye'utz, Sakhyah, and Mirmah. These were his sons, heads of fathers' houses.

11 He also fathered sons by Khushim: Avituv and Elpaal.

12 The sons of Elpaal: Ever, Mish'am, and Shemed, who built Ono and Lod with its towns,

13 And Beriah and Shema, who were heads of fathers' houses of the inhabitants of Ayalon, who drove away the inhabitants of Gat.

14 And Akhyo, Shashak, and Yeremot.

15 Zevadyahu, Arad, Ader,

16 Mikha'el, Yishpah, and Yokha were sons of Beriah.

17 Zevadyahu, Meshullam, Khizki, Khever,

18 Yishmerai, Yizliah, and Yovav were the sons of Elpaal.

19 Yakim, Zikhri, Zavdi,

20 Eli'enai, Tzillethai, Eli'el,

21 Adayahu, Berayahu, and Shimrat were the sons of Shim'i.

22 Yishpan, Ever, Eli'el,

23 Avdon, Zikhri, Khanan,

24 Hananyahu, Eilam, Antotiyah,

25 Yifdeyahu, and Penuel were the sons of Shashak.

26 Shamsherai, Shekharya, Atalyah,

27 Ya'areshyah, Eliyahu, and Zikhri were the sons of Yeroham.

28 These were the heads of fathers' houses, according to their generations, chief men. These lived in Yerushalayim.

29 Ye'iel the father of Giv'on lived in Giv'on, and the name of his wife was Ma'akhah.

30 His firstborn son: Avdon, then Tzur, Kish, Ba'al, Ner, Nadav,

31 Gedor, Akhyo, Zekher,

32 And Miklot, who fathered Shim'ah. Now these also lived opposite their kinsmen in Yerushalayim, with their kinsmen.

33 Ner fathered Kish, Kish fathered Sha'ul, Sha'ul fathered Yonatan, Malkishua, Avinadav, and Eshba'al.

34 The son of Yonatan: Meriv-Ba'al, and Meriv-Ba'al fathered Mikha.

35 The sons of Mikha: Piton, Melekh, Tare'a, and Akaz.

36 Akaz fathered Ye'hora, Ye'hora fathered Alemet, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri fathered Motza.

37 Motza fathered Bin'a. Refayah was his son, El'asah his son, Atzel his son.

38 Atzel had six sons, and these are their names: Azrikam, Bokhru, Yishma'el, She'aryah, Ovadyahu, and Khanan. All these were the sons of Atzel.

39 The sons of his brother Eshek: Ulam his firstborn, Ye'ush the second, and Elifelet the third.

40 The sons of Ulam were mighty warriors, bowmen, having many sons and grandsons, 150. All these were Benjaminites.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 8 is the extended covenant genealogy of the tribe of Binyamin — the covenant tribe of Sha'ul the first covenant king. The covenant genealogy of Sha'ul's line — verses 29-40 — traces the covenant descendants of Sha'ul through Yonatan, Meriv-Ba'al, and their descendants for six more generations. The covenant compiler documents the covenant continuation of the Sha'ulide line alongside the covenant Davidic line.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 33 — Ner fathered Kish, Kish fathered Sha'ul, Sha'ul fathered Yonatan, Malkishua, Avinadav, and Eshba'al. The covenant genealogy of the covenant first king of Yisra'EL is documented with covenant precision. The covenant history of Sha'ul's covenant family is preserved within the covenant national covenant genealogy.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 8 in full. The covenant extended Binyaminite genealogy of this chapter supplements the covenant shorter Binyaminite genealogy of Chapter 7 — providing additional covenant genealogical detail for the covenant tribe that was simultaneously the covenant source of the first covenant king and the covenant ancestral tribe of the covenant Apostle Sha'ul — if the covenant New Testament tradition were being considered, which it is not within this covenant project.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 40 — the sons of Ulam were mighty warriors, bowmen, having many sons and grandsons, 150. The covenant genealogy ends with a covenant quantified covenant portrait of Sha'ul's covenant extended covenant family: mighty, numerous, covenant skilled archers. The covenant descendants of the covenant first king who died in covenant defeat at Gilboa are documented as a covenant vigorous covenant clan in the covenant post-Davidic covenant period.

DIVREI HAYAMIM ALEPH — CHAPTER 9 All Yisra'EL Was Enrolled by Genealogy — Those Who Came Back First — The Priests — The Levites — The Gatekeepers — The Returning Exiles — Sha'ul's Family at Giv'on

1 So all Yisra'EL was enrolled by genealogies, and these are written in the Book of the Kings of Yisra'EL. And Yehudah was taken into exile in Babylon because of their breach of faith.

2 Now the first to dwell again in their possessions in their cities were Yisra'EL, the priests, the Levites, and the temple servants.

3 And some of the people of Yehudah, Binyamin, Efrayim, and Menasheh lived in Yerushalayim:

4 Utai the son of Ammikhud, son of Omri, son of Imri, son of Bani, from the sons of Peretz the son of Yehudah.

5 And of the Shilonites: Asayah the firstborn and his sons.

6 Of the sons of Zerakh: Ye'uel and their kinsmen, 690.

7 Of the Binyaminites: Sallu the son of Meshullam, son of Hodavyah, son of Hassenuah.

8 Yivneyah the son of Yeroham, Elah the son of Uzzi, son of Mikri, and Meshullam the son of Shefatyahu, son of Re'uel, son of Yivniyah.

9 And their kinsmen according to their generations, 956. All these were heads of fathers' houses according to their fathers' houses.

10 Of the priests: Yedayah, Yehoyariv, Yakhin,

11 And Azaryahu the son of Khilkiyahu, son of Meshullam, son of Tzadok, son of Meraioth, son of Akhituv, the chief officer of the house of Creator.

12 And Adayah the son of Yeroham, son of Paskhur, son of Malkiyah, and Ma'asai the son of Adi'el, son of Yakhzerah, son of Meshullam, son of Meshillemit, son of Immer.

13 And their kinsmen, heads of their fathers' houses, 1,760, mighty men for the work of the service of the house of Creator.

14 Of the Levites: Shemayah the son of Khashuv, son of Azrikam, son of Khashavyahu, of the sons of Merari.

15 And Vakbakkar, Kheres, Galal, and Mattanyah the son of Mikha, son of Zikhri, son of Asaf.

16 And Ovadyah the son of Shemayah, son of Galal, son of Yedutun, and Berekhyahu the son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netofathites.

17 The gatekeepers were: Shallum, Akkuv, Talmon, and Akhiman, and their kinsmen — Shallum was the chief.

18 Until then they were in the king's gate on the east side. These were the gatekeepers of the camps of the Levites.

19 Shallum the son of Kore, son of Evyasaf, son of Korakh, and his kinsmen of his fathers' house, the Korahites, were in charge of the work of the service, keepers of the thresholds of the tent, as their fathers had been in charge of the camp of YHWH, keepers of the entrance.

20 And Pinkhas the son of El'azar was the chief over them in former times. YHWH was with him.

21 Zekhariyahu the son of Meshelemyahu was gatekeeper at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

22 All these, who were chosen as gatekeepers at the thresholds, were 212. They were enrolled by genealogies in their villages. Dawid and Shemu'el the seer established them in their office of trust.

23 So they and their sons were in charge of the gates of the house of YHWH, that is, the house of the tent, as guards.

24 The gatekeepers were on the four sides, east, west, north, and south.

25 And their kinsmen who were in their villages were obligated to come in every seven days, in turn, to be with these.

26 For the four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were in charge of the chambers and the treasuries of the house of Creator.

27 And they lodged around the house of Creator, for on them lay the duty of watching, and they had charge of opening it every morning.

28 Some of them had charge of the utensils of service, for they were counted when they were brought in and when they were taken out.

29 Some of them were appointed over the furniture and over all the holy utensils, also over the fine flour, the wine, the oil, the frankincense, and the spices.

30 Others, the sons of the priests, prepared the mixing of the spices.

31 Mattityahu, one of the Levites, the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, was entrusted with making the flat cakes.

32 Also some of their kinsmen of the Kohathites had charge of the showbread, to prepare it every Shabbat.

33 Now these, the singers, the heads of fathers' houses of the Levites, were in the chambers of the temple free from other service, for they were on duty day and night.

34 These were heads of fathers' houses of the Levites, according to their generations, chiefs. These lived in Yerushalayim.

35 In Giv'on lived the father of Giv'on, Ye'uel, and the name of his wife was Ma'akhah.

36 His firstborn son: Avdon, then Tzur, Kish, Ba'al, Ner, Nadav,

37 Gedor, Akhyo, Zekhariah, and Miklot.

38 And Miklot was the father of Shim'am. And these also lived opposite their kinsmen in Yerushalayim, with their kinsmen.

39 Ner was the father of Kish, Kish was the father of Sha'ul, Sha'ul was the father of Yonatan, Malkishua, Avinadav, and Eshba'al.

40 And the son of Yonatan was Meriv-Ba'al, and Meriv-Ba'al was the father of Mikha.

41 The sons of Mikha: Piton, Melekh, Tahrea, and Akaz.

42 And Akaz was the father of Yarah, and Yarah was the father of Alemet, Azmaveth, and Zimri. And Zimri was the father of Motza.

43 Motza was the father of Bin'a, and Refayah was his son, El'asah his son, Atzel his son.

44 Atzel had six sons and these are their names: Azrikam, Bokru, Yishma'el, She'aryah, Ovadyahu, and Khanan. These were the sons of Atzel.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 9 is the covenant transition chapter between the covenant genealogical section — Chapters 1-8 — and the covenant historical narrative — Chapters 10-29. The covenant register of those who returned first to Yerushalayim — verse 2 — is the covenant bridge between the covenant genealogical foundation and the covenant historical action. The covenant people documented in the covenant genealogies become the covenant people who returned to covenant live in the covenant land.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 1 — Yehudah was taken into exile in Babylon because of their breach of faith. The covenant compiler's summary judgment at the transition from covenant genealogy to covenant history: the covenant exile is the covenant consequence of the covenant breach of faith — ma'al — that runs through the covenant genealogical section. The covenant genealogy and the covenant history are connected by the covenant theology of covenant faithfulness and covenant faithlessness.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verses 17-34 provide an extensive covenant record of the covenant gatekeeper families and their covenant responsibilities: watching the threshold, opening the temple morning, maintaining the covenant utensils, preparing the covenant showbread, keeping the covenant spices. The covenant administrative detail of the covenant temple service reflects the covenant compiler's conviction that every covenant function of the covenant sanctuary deserves covenant documentation.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The covenant repetition of the Sha'ulide genealogy in verses 35-44 — paralleling Chapter 8:29-40 — serves the covenant narrative purpose of transitioning from the covenant genealogical section to the covenant Sha'ul narrative of Chapter 10. The covenant death of Sha'ul at Gilboa opens the covenant Dawid narrative. The covenant genealogical repetition prepares the covenant reader for the covenant historical transition.

END OF PART ONE — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 9
